NKJV.1: When Israel went out of Egypt, The house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
NKJV.2: Judah became His sanctuary, And Israel His dominion.
NKJV.3: The sea saw it and fled; Jordan turned back.
NKJV.4: The mountains skipped like rams, The little hills like lambs.
NKJV.5: What ails you, O sea, that you fled? O Jordan, that you turned back?
NKJV.6: O mountains, that you skipped like rams? O little hills, like lambs?
NKJV.7: Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord, At the presence of the God of Jacob,
NKJV.8: Who turned the rock into a pool of water, The flint into a fountain of waters.

ESV.1: When Israel went out from Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
ESV.2: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his dominion.
ESV.3: The sea looked and fled; Jordan turned back.
ESV.4: The mountains skipped like rams, the hills like lambs.
ESV.5: What ails you, O sea, that you flee? O Jordan, that you turn back?
ESV.6: O mountains, that you skip like rams? O hills, like lambs?
ESV.7: Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob,
ESV.8: who turns the rock into a pool of water, the flint into a spring of water.

NASB.1: When Israel went forth from Egypt, The house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
NASB.2: Judah became His sanctuary, Israel, His dominion.
NASB.3: The sea looked and fled; The Jordan turned back.
NASB.4: The mountains skipped like rams, The hills, like lambs.
NASB.5: What ails you, O sea, that you flee? O Jordan, that you turn back?
NASB.6: O mountains, that you skip like rams? O hills, like lambs?
NASB.7: Tremble, O earth, before the Lord, Before the God of Jacob,
NASB.8: Who turned the rock into a pool of water, The flint into a fountain of water.

NLT.1:
When the Israelites escaped from Egypt —
when the family of Jacob left that foreign land —
NLT.2:
the land of Judah became God’s sanctuary,
and Israel became his kingdom.
NLT.3:
The Red Sea saw them coming and hurried out of their way!
The water of the Jordan River turned away.
NLT.4:
The mountains skipped like rams,
the hills like lambs!
NLT.5:
What’s wrong, Red Sea, that made you hurry out of their way?
What happened, Jordan River, that you turned away?
NLT.6:
Why, mountains, did you skip like rams?
Why, hills, like lambs?
NLT.7:
Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord,
at the presence of the God of Jacob.
NLT.8:
He turned the rock into a pool of water;
yes, a spring of water flowed from solid rock.

WEB.1: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of foreign language;
WEB.2: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his dominion.
WEB.3: The sea saw it, and fled. The Jordan was driven back.
WEB.4: The mountains skipped like rams, the little hills like lambs.
WEB.5: What was it, you sea, that you fled? You Jordan, that you turned back?
WEB.6: You mountains, that you skipped like rams; you little hills, like lambs?
WEB.7: Tremble, you earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob,
WEB.8: who turned the rock into a pool of water, the flint into a spring of waters.

AMP.1:
When Israel came out of Egypt,
The house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
AMP.2:
Judah became His sanctuary,
And Israel His dominion.
AMP.3:
The [Red] Sea looked and fled;
The Jordan turned back.
AMP.4:
The mountains leaped like rams,
The [little] hills, like lambs.
AMP.5:
What ails you, O sea, that you flee?
O Jordan, that you turn back?
AMP.6:
O mountains, that you leap like rams,
O [little] hills, like lambs?
AMP.7:
Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord,
At the presence of the God of Jacob (Israel),
AMP.8:
Who turned the rock into a pool of water,
The flint into a fountain of water.

MSG.1:
After Israel left Egypt,
the clan of Jacob left those barbarians behind;
MSG.2:
Judah became holy land for him,
Israel the place of holy rule.
MSG.3:
Sea took one look and ran the other way;
River Jordan turned around and ran off.
MSG.4:
The mountains turned playful and skipped like rams,
the hills frolicked like spring lambs.
MSG.5:
What's wrong with you, Sea, that you ran away?
and you, River Jordan, that you turned and ran off?
MSG.6:
And mountains, why did you skip like rams?
and you, hills, frolic like spring lambs?
MSG.7:
Tremble, Earth! You're in the Lord's presence!
in the presence of Jacob's God.
MSG.8:
He turned the rock into a pool of cool water,
turned flint into fresh spring water.

NIV.1:
Psalm 114
When Israel came out of Egypt,
the house of Jacob from a people of foreign tongue,
NIV.2:
Judah became God’s sanctuary,
Israel his dominion.
NIV.3:
The sea looked and fled,
the Jordan turned back;
NIV.4:
the mountains skipped like rams,
the hills like lambs.
NIV.5:
Why was it, O sea, that you fled,
O Jordan, that you turned back,
NIV.6:
you mountains, that you skipped like rams,
you hills, like lambs?
NIV.7:
Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord,
at the presence of the God of Jacob,
NIV.8:
who turned the rock into a pool,
the hard rock into springs of water.

NRSVCE.1:
When Israel went out from Egypt,
the house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
NRSVCE.2:
Judah became God’s sanctuary,

NRSVCE.3:
The sea looked and fled;
Jordan turned back.
NRSVCE.4:
The mountains skipped like rams,
the hills like lambs.
NRSVCE.5:
Why is it, O sea, that you flee?

NRSVCE.6:

NRSVCE.7:

who turns the rock into a pool of water,

NET.1: When Israel left Egypt, when the family of Jacob left a foreign nation behind,
NET.2: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his kingdom.
NET.3: The sea looked and fled; the Jordan River turned back.
NET.4: The mountains skipped like rams, the hills like lambs.
NET.5: Why do you flee, O sea? Why do you turn back, O Jordan River?
NET.6: Why do you skip like rams, O mountains, like lambs, O hills?
NET.7: Tremble, O earth, before the Lord — before the God of Jacob,
NET.8: who turned a rock into a pool of water, a hard rock into springs of water!
